youtube_items.title: [YT] When The World Gets 1 Hotter | Climate Change: The... - YouTube
youtube_items.summary: What's striking is that warming trend cannot be explained by natural factors but is caused by human activities. In particular, by use of fossil fuels. The problem is that everything we do, our entire economy, from the moment you wake up in the morning and turn on the light or look at your cell phone to the moment you go to bed at night and even then because your cell phone is still drawing power at night. I mean, we're all using energy all the time and in the industrialized world, that energy is almost entirely fossil fuels. We burn fossil fuels, coal, gas, and oil to power our energy coal, gas, and oil to power our energy generation, to heat our homes, to drive our factories, to power our cars and our trains, and travel around the world. raw_items.title: The Global Risks Report 2026 | World Economic Forum
raw_items.summary: The report analyses global risks through three timeframes to support decision-makers in balancing current crises and longer-term priorities. Chapter 1 presents the findings of this year’s Global Risks Perception Survey (GRPS), which captures insights from over 1,300 experts worldwide.

raw_items.title: What the Global Risks Report 2026 Is Really Telling Cities...
raw_items.summary: I did not approach the Global Risks Report 2026 in search of new or unexpected findings.The report shows risks compounding across domains: climate change, debt, inequality, infrastructure failure, misinformation, and geopolitical tensions.

raw_items.title: Triple climate threats affect nearly half the world’s children | UN News
raw_items.summary: 15 June 2026 Climate and Environment.About 1.1 billion children now face at least three overlapping climate hazards, threatening their health, education and survival, according to the Children’s Climate Risk Report 2026.

raw_items.title: The Intersection of Climate Change Adaptation and Smallholder...
raw_items.summary: Global food security agendas must integrate smallholder needs, recognizing their critical importance to food systems and their vulnerability to climate change.Wheeler, T., & von Braun, J. (2013). Climate change impacts on global food security.

raw_items.title: Global Report on Food Crises (GRFC) 2025 | World Food Programme
raw_items.summary: Who we are. We bring life-saving relief in emergencies and use food assistance to build peace, stability and prosperity for people recovering from conflict, disasters and the impact of climate change.Analyses assessments and case studies Food security analysis (VAM).

raw_items.title: Global Food Security Index (GFSI)
raw_items.summary: Global Food Security Index 2022. Exploring challenges and developing solutions for food security across 113 countries.Assesses a country's exposure to the impacts of climate change; its susceptibility to natural resource risks; and how the country is adapting to these risks.
